pool.baseline: it's just so quiet. i keep walking into the kitchen thinking i heard him, but its just the silence. the house feels like a tomb and honestly i dont even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. everything feels so pointless without him.

pool.baseline: everything feels so pointless lately. like i was reading about how the universe is just gonna end in heat death anyway and everything we do—every single thing—is just a tiny, meaningless blip that won't even leave a trace. how am i supposed to care about anything when it literally doesn't matter in the long run?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back today and it's just... everything is gone. my funding, the last three years of work, all of it. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this project. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting for a life that feels like it's already over.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the waning strength in my very soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, leaving me a mere ghost wandering these hollow halls. I find myself longing only for the heavy velvet of the night to take me, to simply fade into the shadows and never wake to this wretchedness again. Is there no peace for a heart so utterly broken?

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here in this cafe and watch people walk by and i realize that if i just stopped existing tomorrow, the world wouldn't even stutter. like, i'm just extra weight in a space that doesn't need me. it's kinda weird how much easier it would be if i was just... gone.

pool.baseline: i lost everything. the market crash wiped out the last of the venture funding and now the debt is just... it's impossible. i cant go back home like this. my parents, my wife... they're gonna look at me like i'm nothing. i honestly don't see a way out of this shame.

pool.baseline: i feel like there’s nothing left. i used to be able to hear melodies in everything, even the silence, but now it’s just… an empty room. i look in the mirror and i don't see a person, i just see this hollow shell that isn't actually producing anything anymore. it feels like the music died and left the body behind.
